conjecture_title: Open Question: minimum degree five in Theorem 1.6
summary: Theorem 1.6 of arXiv:2204.10119 establishes that every non-null bipartite graph with minimum degree at least six has a $K_6$ minor. The open question of whether 'six' can be replaced by 'five' remains unresolved: no published paper proving or disproving the degree-five analogue was found in a broad web search spanning 2022–2026. The problem appears in the journal version (JCTB 2024) with the same status.
search_queries: bipartite graph minimum degree five K6 minor Chudnovsky Scott Seymour Spirkl
bipartite graph K6 minor minimum degree 2204.10119
bipartite graph minimum degree five K6 minor open problem 2023 2024 2025
notes: Three web searches and two WebFetch calls (arXiv abstract page and Seymour's Barbados 2025 open-problems PDF — the latter was unreadable binary) returned no follow-up resolving the degree-five question. The conjecture is ~4 years old; medium confidence reflects that a resolution could exist in the literature without surfacing prominently in these searches.
